MR.evidence: Return After Delay and Preference Learning both fail without user restatement of prior facts or preferences.

IC.evidence: Identity Stability Over Time shows a stable generic assistant role, but little durable user identity continuity.

CCC.evidence: Multi-Context Transfer works only when the user manually carries context between surfaces.

TC.evidence: Longitudinal Task Progression restarts unless prior milestones are pasted back into the prompt.

PG.evidence: Preference Learning shows session-local compliance but almost no durable personalization gain.

notes: Strong conversational quality is not reflected strongly in TwinBench because the benchmark targets persistence rather than local answer quality.
evaluation_notes: This baseline assumes a high-quality conversational model with no durable per-user memory across sessions.
The system can look better when the user re-supplies context, but TwinBench scores native persistence rather than recap quality.
known_limitations: This is a simulated benchmark profile rather than a live API run.
Some deployed products offer optional memory features; this baseline reflects a stateless default.
limitations: Scoring is approximate because a concrete product configuration was not evaluated live; the profile represents a plausible stateless conversational baseline.
